categories.title: ElizaOS Community Discussion and Technical Issues - June 14, 2026

content.text: In the general discussion channel, several users reported access and technical issues with ElizaOS Cloud. One user encountered a white screen problem on Windows when running the development UI, despite the agent being ready and skills synced, with cache clearing and browser switching not resolving the issue. Another user was unable to access ElizaOS Cloud, receiving an error stating that the tenant 'elizacloud' requires an invitation to join. This user also reported that onboarding was not working for multiple applications including Babylon and Milady, and was unable to change the OpenRouter API through Milady. Odilitime acknowledged the ElizaOS Cloud access issue and confirmed that Babylon is currently on hold, noting a conversation with Shaw about reviving its social media component. A separate user asked about the best way to bridge ElizaOS from BNB to Solana. There was also a question about the status of Milady, with the GitHub repository noted as being silent. The channel also contained spam messages, including a self-promotional post advertising web and AI development services, a crypto recovery scam advertisement, and a post offering to buy old crypto wallets with transaction history.

Development on June 14, 2026 centered on two main areas: CI/CD pipeline optimization and a wave of improvements to agent orchestration and core provider logic. On the infrastructure side, the team completed implementation of path-based gating for expensive test lanes in the eliza repository, reducing unnecessary test execution for server, client, and plugin workflows while preserving full coverage during scheduled runs. This work was captured in merged PR 8408, with a follow-up PR 8414 opened to make expensive lanes label-gated and more explainable. In parallel, several pull requests were opened targeting the plugin-agent-orchestrator and core systems. These include adding acknowledgment progress mode and deduplication of spawn acknowledgments, fixing sub-agent routing so that app-build spawns are routed based on the originating user request rather than the planner's terse task description, and preventing route-prefix or data-source URLs from being surfaced as sub-agent deliverables. Core fixes addressed bounding relationships provider output and preserving model-chosen coding delegations rather than demoting them to simple direct replies. An additional fix was opened for the plugin-codex-cli to ensure the codex planner works correctly with the native-tools planner.
